interactions.summary: Jiang uses Sparta, gangs, cartels, and child armies to describe a trauma-bonding model in which abused children become fiercely loyal to the figure who later gives them belonging.
He presents the Assassins as a secret-society example where hallucinogens, sex, trauma, paradise promises, and early training produce obedient killers and spies.
A student asks why the traumatized woman would not remember who killed her family and hate the murderer rather than rely on him.
Jiang answers with dissociation: trauma removes memory and leaves a hidden handler who can later command obedience like someone holding a password.

speaker_notes.note: This is the only clear non-Jiang substantive question in this packet.

claims.claim: Jiang presents Spartan education as a trauma-bonding system that turns abused children into soldiers loyal to older male commanders.
The same abuse-then-belonging principle is said to operate in gangs, cartels, child armies, and secret societies.
Jiang's model of the Assassins combines drugs, sex, paradise imagery, trauma, and early training to produce perfect slaves, spies, and killers.
Trauma can create dissociation, leaving the victim obedient to a handler whose role is not consciously remembered.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's answer to the student question

signature_moments.moment: The general becomes the soldier's rescuer precisely because he arrives inside the wound.
signature_moments.source_phrase: he saved my life when I most needed it
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It captures the dark logic of obedience as gratitude after abandonment.

signature_moments.moment: Sex plus trauma plus hallucinogens create 'perfect slaves.'
signature_moments.source_phrase: sex plus trauma, they create the perfect slaves
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is one of the lecture's most compact and disturbing formulas.

signature_moments.moment: The handler is forgotten but still holds the password.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the trauma model a memorable mechanism for unconscious obedience.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang uses the term for repeated institutionalized childhood trauma, with Sparta as his example.
A historical secret society Jiang uses to illustrate drugs, paradise promises, and obedient killing.
A trauma response where the person is 'not there' and cannot remember the event in detail.
The hidden controller in Jiang's programming model, remembered unconsciously rather than openly.

uncertainty_notes.note: ASR renders hashish as 'hashes'; normalize conceptually as hashish/hallucinogen if used in public prose.
